seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Three Springs and Tibradden. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Three Springs vs Tibradden: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Three Springs and Tibradden.
heading: Three Springs vs Tibradden — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Three Springs, Western Australia and Tibradden, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Three Springs has a lower overall climate risk score (31/100) compared to Tibradden (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Three Springs or Tibradden safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Three Springs has a flood risk score of 41/100, while Tibradden scores 40/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Three Springs scores 29/100 for bushfire risk. Tibradden scores 63/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Three Springs or Tibradden?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Three Springs has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.83% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
